title: Canonical fact object
description: The fact-object shape the library's fill paths accept ({matter, parties, party, facts} — docs/integrations/README.md). mapping.json values are dotted paths into this object; engine/canonical.py translates it to the engine case shape for recipe-tier forms. Validate with tools/preflight.py (also exposed as the MCP lint_case tool), which adds suggestion-bearing checks (e.g. parties.lawyer -> parties.attorney) beyond what JSON Schema expresses.

matter.description: The case/filing context. All values are strings; dates are ISO yyyy-mm-dd (the filler renders mm/dd/yyyy).

parties.description: Role -> party. Known roles: plaintiff (also the petitioner side), defendant (also the respondent side), attorney, other_party (role-neutral opponent), child_1..child_N (numbered minor children).

party.description: The single filing party (the person signing). Used by forms that don't fix the filer's caption role.

facts.description: Form-specific facts; keys are per-form (see each form's mapping.json "facts".used). Values are scalars or string lists.

generic.description: Sample-case metadata: true marks a generic placeholder sample not tailored to the form.

generic_reason.description: Sample-case metadata: why a generic sample could not be tailored (tools/gen_sample_cases.py records the rejection reason here).
